$comment: Machine-readable capability status. Every advertised capability must appear here. 'implemented' requires implementation + correctness tests + verification; anything less is 'partial' or 'absent'. Checked in CI.

verification: Wasmtime 36 LTS engine (patched line per RUSTSEC advisories); typed+dynamic invocation asserted on concrete values; traps, fuel exhaustion, epoch timeouts, and memory limits mapped to typed BarqError variants; WASI stdout captured and asserted
CLI: run fixtures/add.wasm --invoke add --arg-i32 20 --arg-i32 22 returns 42
capabilities.notes: Backed by Wasmtime 36 LTS, not a from-scratch engine. Fuel/timeout are opt-in via RuntimeConfig.

capabilities.capability: native AVX2/FMA kernels (dot product, L2 norm, cosine, quantize, dequantize, matmul)
correctness_tests: tests/kernel_differential.rs (differential vs scalar, executed on AVX2 hosts e.g. CI x86-64)
benchmarks: run scripts: cargo run --release --features bench-tool --bin barq-bench on an AVX2 host; no curated x86 result file is checked in yet
verification: scripts/verify-native-simd.sh asserts vmulps/vaddps/vfmadd/ymm in emitted x86-64 assembly (CI step)
Runtime detection via is_x86_feature_detected; safe wrappers refuse unsupported CPUs with typed errors
capabilities.notes: Executed and differentially tested on x86-64 CI runners; on non-AVX2 hosts the same tests verify typed-error refusal.

capabilities.capability: native NEON kernels (dot product, L2 norm, cosine, quantize, dequantize, matmul)
correctness_tests: tests/kernel_differential.rs (differential vs scalar, executed on aarch64)
verification: scripts/verify-native-simd.sh asserts fmla/ld1/.4s in emitted aarch64 assembly (verified on Apple M4)
Runtime detection via is_aarch64_feature_detected

correctness_tests: tests/browser.rs (10 headless-Chrome tests with simd128: differential dot/norm/cosine, bit-exact quantize incl. NaN/inf/ties, dequantize, elementwise, repeated invocation, 500k-element transfer; 3 tests in scalar configuration)
verification: scripts/verify-wasm-simd.sh: simd bundle must contain v128.load/f32x4.mul/f32x4.add/i32x4.trunc_sat_f32x4_s and *_simd128 exports; scalar bundle must contain zero SIMD ops and no *_simd128 exports (checked with the wasmparser-based wasm-inspect binary)
docs/browser/loader.js feature-detects SIMD before loading and cross-checks simd128_enabled()
capabilities.notes: Two bundles: pkg/scalar and pkg/simd. Firefox headless run wired in CI.

verification: OnceLock-cached is_x86_feature_detected!/is_aarch64_feature_detected!; BARQ_FORCE_KERNEL override returns typed error for unsupported backends

correctness_tests: tests/analyzer_tests.rs (labeled WAT corpus: precision=1.0 and recall=1.0 asserted; near-misses rejected; fabricated byte arrays refused)
verification: wasmparser-based parsing of imports/exports/code; symbolic execution reconstructs load/store address expressions, accumulators, and induction variables
Candidates require mandatory structural evidence (e.g. multiply-accumulate over two distinct base pointers); confidence = satisfied requirements / total, verified in tests to never be a constant
capabilities.notes: Detects dot product, matrix multiply, and quantization candidates. Detection only — no execution substitution until Phase 6.

capabilities.capability: specialization: barq host-kernel ABI + Cranelift JIT (narrow signatures)
correctness_tests: tests/specialization_tests.rs (6 tests: host vs pure-guest vs native differential, bit-exact quantize into guest memory, OOB and misalignment traps, ABI-disabled instantiation failure)
tests/jit_tests.rs (5 tests: compiled dot product vs scalar across lengths, 200-case randomized lengths, typed errors, multi-instance memory safety)
verification: Host kernels route through the same verified dispatch (AVX2/FMA, NEON, scalar); guests opt in by importing the barq module — nothing is silently rewritten
JIT builds complete Cranelift IR functions (loop with block params), finalizes real executable memory, typed wrappers validate lengths; no test asserts machine_code.len() > 0
capabilities.notes: Automatic substitution of detected patterns is intentionally NOT implemented; analyzer output only recommends the opt-in ABI.

capabilities.capability: scalar compute kernels (dot product, L2 norm, cosine similarity, matrix multiply, conv2d, quantization, elementwise ops, statistics)
correctness_tests: tests/kernel_differential.rs (scalar references are the ground truth)
verification: Differential tests against naive scalar references; no SIMD or auto-vectorization claims made
capabilities.notes: Partial because not every exported function has a dedicated test yet and no reproducible benchmarks exist.
lz4_compress_experimental returns input verbatim under 128 KiB and emits an unvalidated LZ4-like format above; no decompressor, no format conformance test.

verification: conv2d_ex reports KernelBackend::Scalar truthfully
capabilities.notes: conv2d dispatch never claims SIMD; a vectorized conv2d is future work.
avx512f is detected and reported but no AVX-512 kernel exists.

capabilities.capability: reproducible benchmarks (native harness + criterion + browser page)
correctness_tests: tests/bench_integrity.rs (5 tests: incorrect implementations publish no timings; nonzero real durations; environment metadata recorded; no sleeps/fabricated durations in benchmark sources; checked-in JSON validated)
benchmarks: benchmarks/results/apple-m4-macos.json (98 workload records, Apple M4, macOS, neon backend, git commit + environment embedded)
verification: Every workload verifies its result against the scalar reference before timing; barq-bench exits non-zero if any candidate is incorrect
Size sweep 15..1_000_000 and matrices 16..256; results consumed via black_box; warmup + median/p90/p95/stddev recorded
capabilities.notes: Browser page measures JS baseline vs wasm scalar/unrolled/simd128 with identical methodology; browser numbers are not published automatically.

correctness_tests: tests/fuzz_smoke.rs (randomized/mutated modules and hostile float inputs never panic)
cargo xtask verify-all runs every verification layer and reports PASS/FAIL/NOT RUN
verification: CI matrix: Linux x86-64, macOS ARM64, Windows x86-64 (build+tests); release SIMD tests + assembly verification on x86-64 and arm64; browser tests (Chrome both bundles, Firefox simd) with binary instruction verification; MSRV 1.87 check; docs build; benchmark integrity; truthfulness grep + status-file consistency gate
Non-blocking signal jobs: cargo-audit, AddressSanitizer over kernel tests
verify-all writes verification-report.json/.md; NOT RUN is never reported as PASS
capabilities.notes: Coverage reporting and Miri are not wired into CI yet; nothing claims they are.
